name: RC522 NFC/RFID
description: The `rc522_spi` component allows you to use RC522 NFC/RFID controllers with ESPHome. This component is a global hub that establishes the connection to the RC522 via [SPI](https://esphome.io/components/spi). Using [RC522 binary sensors](https://esphome.io/components/binary_sensor/rc522#rc522-binary_sensor), you can then create individual binary sensors that track if an NFC/RFID tag is currently detected by the RC522.

config_entries.label: Update Interval
config_entries.description: The duration of each scan on the RC522. This affects the duration that the individual binary sensors stay active when they're found. If a device is not found within this time window, it will be marked as not present. Defaults to `1s`.

config_entries.label: CS Pin
config_entries.description: The pin on the ESP that the chip select line is connected to.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Reset Pin
config_entries.description: The pin connected to the RST line. Some tests shows the RC522 working okay without this.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: SPI ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID of the [SPI Component](https://esphome.io/components/spi) if you want to use multiple SPI buses.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for this component.
